Mr.. g. Yoshioka, late of Stanford
university and manager of the Orien-
tai Trading company, is in Spokane
for the purpose of organizing a
branch office Of that company. Other
branch offices are already established
at Kalispell and Harvard, with head
quarters at Seattle. The company
supplies the railroads with Japanese
laborers, of whom nearly 5000 are
already working for the Great North
ern and Northern Pacific roads


Mr.. Yoshioka, 204 Post street, is
well known as a political economist
and author. When seen yesterday by
a Press man the little gentleman was
very pleased to express himself con-
corning the trouble brewing over the
orient. He said:


whatever it was that prompted
the Russian emperor to advocate the
peace conference at The Hague, GER
tain 1t is that the Russian policy is
disturbing the entire world, involv-
ing every power. The integrity OF
the mammoth empire is about to be
shattered, perhaps swallowed up.


I'VE denounce the Russian art Of
